result:
CodeGraph indexes source symbols, not build files, so I verified the build-system chain directly via grep ŌĆö confirmed and complete. Here's the full picture:

## (a) Kconfig option

**`drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:58-76`**
```
58: config E1000E
59:     tristate "Intel(R) PRO/1000 PCI-Express Gigabit Ethernet support"
60:     depends on PCI && (!SPARC32 || BROKEN)
61:     depends on PTP_1588_CLOCK_OPTIONAL
62:     select CRC32
```
(A related option, `E1000E_HWTS` at line 78, depends on `E1000E && X86` for HW cross-timestamp support ŌĆö not required to build the driver itself.)

## (b) Driver-local Makefile rule

**`drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile:11-15`**
```
11: obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e.o
12:
13: e1000e-objs := 82571.o ich8lan.o 80003es2lan.o \
14:                mac.o manage.o nvm.o phy.o \
15:                param.o ethtool.o netdev.o ptp.o
```

## (c) Full build-system gating chain (top ŌåÆ driver)

CONFIG_E1000E is checked **twice** on the way down ŌĆö once to descend into the `e1000e/` directory, once to actually build the object inside it.

| Layer | File:line | Gate |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Kernel build core | `drivers/Makefile:95` | `obj-y += net/` ŌĆö unconditional descent |
| 2. Net drivers | `drivers/net/Makefile:52` | `obj-$(CONFIG_ETHERNET) += ethernet/` |
| 3. Ethernet vendors | `drivers/net/ethernet/Makefile:50` | `obj-$(CONFIG_NET_VENDOR_INTEL) += intel/` |
| 4. Intel drivers | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Makefile:8` | `obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e/` (descend) |
| 5. Driver-local | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile:11` | `obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e.o` (compile) |

Matching Kconfig gates that must all resolve `y`/`m` for `CONFIG_E1000E` to even be selectable:

- `drivers/net/Kconfig:27` ŌĆö `if NETDEVICES` (`menuconfig NETDEVICES` depends on `NET`, defined at `drivers/net/Kconfig:6-9`), closed at `drivers/net/Kconfig:647`.
- `drivers/net/Kconfig:490` ŌĆö `source "drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig"` (inside the `NETDEVICES` block above).
- `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:6-13` ŌĆö `menuconfig ETHERNET` (bool, `depends on NET`, `default y`) ŌåÆ `if ETHERNET` opens the block (closes at `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:196`). This is what backs `CONFIG_ETHERNET` used in gate #2 above.
- `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:86` ŌĆö `source "drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig"` (inside the `ETHERNET` block).
- `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:6-8` ŌĆö `config NET_VENDOR_INTEL` (bool, `default y`) ŌåÆ `if NET_VENDOR_INTEL` opens at line 17, closes at line 359. This backs gate #3 above and wraps `config E1000E` itself.

So the effective condition to build e1000e.o is: `NET` Ōł¦ `NETDEVICES` Ōł¦ `ETHERNET` Ōł¦ `NET_VENDOR_INTEL` Ōł¦ `E1000E` (with `E1000E` further requiring `PCI`, `!SPARC32 || BROKEN`, `PTP_1588_CLOCK_OPTIONAL`), and the directory-descent chain re-checks the same `CONFIG_ETHERNET` / `CONFIG_NET_VENDOR_INTEL` / `CONFIG_E1000E` flags at each Makefile level (5 layers total) before the driver-local Makefile compiles the object list.
